item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Oh yea, woops.

Fixed.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Wait, can you fix the tense for the part I suggested upthread to:

Addendum 1501-D: [Leaked data from Manna Charitable Foundation Leaked data from Manna Charitable Foundation] suggests that several SCP-1501 instances will be relocated to a future MCF facility at Ontario, Canada. If the existence of the facility is verified, SCP-1501 instances are to be intercepted.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I like this as well. I'll take it. Thanks for the suggestion.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
While a general link to MCF is amiable, I think a link to a specific MCF article can be amiable as well. From my understanding, they seem to be repurposing 1501 instances in Draft Proposal For Programme Arduinosis 9001.

Thus, I suggest this:

Addendum 1501-D: Leaked data from Manna Charitable Foundation suggests that several SCP-1501 instances are relocated to a future MCF facility at Ontario, Canada. If existence of the facility is verified, SCP-1501 instances are to be intercepted.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Stopped. Edits Made.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

I propose the following edit:

SCP-1501 is a set of humanoid automatons, constructed of copper, iron, plastic, and animal hair. The words "Manna Charitable Foundation Official Charity Delegate" have been embossed into the back of every SCP-1501 instance.

To this:

SCP-1501 is a set of humanoid automatons, constructed of copper, iron, plastic, and animal hair. The words "Manna Charitable Foundation Official Charity Delegate" have been embossed into the back of every SCP-1501 instance.

And make this addition:

Addendum 1501-D: Leaked data from Manna Charitable Foundation suggests that several SCP-1501 instances will be relocated to a future MCF facility at Ontario, Canada. If the existence of the facility is verified, SCP-1501 instances are to be intercepted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the beginning bit, the idea of robots pretending to be humans and stalking you for weeks, then pestering you with targeted mail for charities is actually very creepy.

But the 'impersonating your loved ones' bit is cartoonish and unnecessary, it ruins any creepiness that the rest provided, and just makes them seem incompetent.

I think a change you could make that would make it a bit better is to have the robots call the target in their loved ones voice, but maintain just enough roboticness to fall into the uncanny valley (have them impersonate the voice perfectly, but have the weird pauses between words like text-to-speech programs do). have them camp outside peoples houses to harass them whenever they attempt to leave their houses if you need a more physical element. but having them wear incompetent disguises is just… ugh.

"Well no wonder that Loch Ness Monster SCP-1501 keeps coming back to our house, you keep givin' em tree fiddy!"

I really like the way this plays up the dynamic of how you might expect to give just a one-time donation, or that maybe if you give a bit more money then it'll leave you alone. But no, now you're identified as a 'preferred donor' and get targeted relentlessly. No good deed goes unpunished, eh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading an inferior version of this. This version, however, I really like. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the MCF in this article. They're just so…so earnest in their desire to help using skips, despite the fact that it goes horribly, horribly wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea actually rather plain. And yet I absolutely love it. The way they start stalking their targets, kidnapping loved ones and breaking into their homes….it's just lovely.

Also, with the letter at the end, Christ should be capitalized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You wrote this on my birthday, and I approve of that.

Also the automaton is fun, too.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My brain wants to connect this with 1377, the automatons that try to infiltrate legislatures but the builders just don't "get" humanity.

1501s seem to be the slightly improved version 1.5. Still don't pass for human beyond casual inspection, and still prone to word salad when they try to use language beyond a rigid script; but the mysterious builders have obviously made the right call in enrolling the MCF as beta testers. It's good that MCF is managing to help someone, even if it's not the people they meant to help…and there was some home invasion in the process…and the benficiaries almost certainly aren't human…and are trying to do something with the world's governments….

Y'know what? Maybe the MCF should stop trying to be so damn helpful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem with this one is that you didn't really clarify what foundation was involved. Also, I thought that the SCP Foundation never managed to find an actual Manna site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely lost it at "KIND, BRAVE, HANDSOME STALLION". Overall though, this was pretty decent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. Good intentions gone awry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ach, so much flak for the reference. I'll remove it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aaagh, Roget, why did you have to include the honey reference? ;n; I actually don't wanna upvote this now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hopefully, this crane we got to clean up will be the last time we have to deal with them.

http://knowyourmeme.com/memes/usually-takes-a-crane ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have successfully captured that horrible awkwardness when someone comes up to you asking for money. I like it.

I don't really mind the impersonation stuff, but it could be removed without changing my opinion on the the piece. I do agree on Smapti's point. There's no real need to bring up the honey thing, and it serves to needlessly hammer home the "MCF are incompetent" angle, which is an interpretation I don't really agree with in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm almost certain that "Foundation facilities" in this context refers to the Manna Charitable Foundation, not the SCP Foundation. Hopefully the author will be by at some point to offer clarification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've got a bit of a redundancy here:

repeatedly donates to an individual instance of SCP-1501 multiple times

And I agree with what's been said about the impersonation stuff: it doesn't really fit the tone of the rest of the article (which, I might add, is otherwise excellent). The addenda are both great, but the interview is like something out of a million horror films, what with the mirror reveal and the repeating itself and whatnot. I still like the core idea enough to upvote, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that the spouse replacement stuff is a little forced- at least if it happens all the time- but otherwise this is well-executed and seems like a novel concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So am I missing something or does the article ever explain what the instance do with the spouses while they are disguised? Are they just there while the spouse is out of the house, do they have them tied up somewhere, or mysteriously disappear?

I would argue though that the spouse disguise tactic if removed without other alterations would make the article much weaker. Reason being that the interview in my opinion is one of the article's better bits, so you'd have to come up with a replacement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How would they thow about under-performing Foundation facilities? I assume all the 'front' organisations report cooked books, presenting 'normal' income and expenditure levels so they don't draw undue attention. Or is that the reaso for Euclid status: SCP-1501 is able to glean confidential SCP information?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had an idea for MCF bellringers a few months ago, but dropped it because I couldn't come up with a good hook.

I endorse this thoroughly.

I feel like it could stand to lose the line about "the honey fiasco", though. Not every MCF SCP needs to be compared to SCP-1176, and it's not something that the world at large would know about - just the MCF itself, the Foundation, possibly the GOC, and some Ethiopians.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even in articles not my own, the curse of the unwanted "R" continues to haunt me. Will I ever be free of it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Observed collections have been varied, with collections for endangered species of birds, poverty, AIDS, and under-performing Foundation facilities

You might want to clarify if this is supposed to be MCF or SCP Foundation facilities. Although, the idea of MCF forming charities to support Foundation sites is actually kinda amusing.

And I agree with Dmatrix. I think you could stand to lose the whole "disguise themselves as loved ones" part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. I like the idea, and I quite liked the article, up until the whole "wax prosthetics" business. It seems like a somewhat unnecessarily cartoonish and unrealistic scare tactic for the article, when the idea of being pestered by the automaton forever is more unsettling on its own. For now, no vote.
